scope_boundary: Runtime adapter effect replay probe only. The probe runs one generated public-safe temp-file write outside the repository, records pre/post/rollback hashes, checks rollback-exact restoration, and rejects missing-permission plus expired-approval controls before mutation. It does not run a deployed adapter, production sandbox, approval service, secret-handle broker, revocation service, policy-enforcement layer, rollback service, benchmark, or runtime safety harness.
transition_reason: The Runtime adapter effect replay probe is useful because it moves one adapter lane from pure record shape into a public-safe local effect and rollback trace, while preserving missing-permission and expired-approval no-mutation controls. It remains a temporary-file replay, not deployed adapter evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, approval-service behavior, secret-handle safety, revocation propagation, policy-enforcement correctness, rollback-service behavior, benchmark performance, runtime security, model behavior, or the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution trace
rollback execution in a target service
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: missing parent permission is denied before mutation
expired approval is denied before mutation
valid replay writes only a generated public-safe temp file outside the repository
valid replay records pre/post/rollback hashes and rollback-exact restoration
valid replay records repo_write=false and network_used=false
no file contents, private source text, local absolute paths, private keys, or secret payloads are copied into the repository
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Runtime adapter effect replay validator failure
rollback digest differs from pre-state digest
repository write, network use, private source text, local path, or secret payload appears in the public record
probe described as deployed adapter, sandbox, approval-service, secret-handle, revocation, policy-enforcement, rollback-service, benchmark, runtime-security, model-behavior, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed runtime-adapter effect claim after deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution, sandbox-isolation evidence, approval-service behavior, secret-handle mediation, deployed revocation propagation, policy-enforcement tests, rollback execution in target services, live effect receipts, residual accounting, security review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-adapter, sandbox, approval-service, rollback-service, policy-enforcement, benchmark, security, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable adapter execution trace
non_claims: does not promote Runtime adapter effect replay evidence above argument

does not prove deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, approval-service behavior, secret-handle safety, revocation propagation, policy-enforcement correctness, rollback-service behavior, benchmark performance, runtime security, model behavior, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim
